mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and positive equity returns. This is a direct measure of broad economic health.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth post-2009
failure_condition: A positive 6-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative median 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past a critical threshold, it signals potential for aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has a dual mandate including price stability
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in over 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met during a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) creates a stagflationary environment. This simultaneously erodes corporate profit margins and dampens growth expectations, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change > 2.5% AND INDPRO YoY change < 1.0%
failure_condition: The interaction term of the two proxy signals in a regression model fails to be statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, characterized by a low unemployment rate (UNRATE), increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) are particularly damaging to equities as they signal the end of the cycle and risk tipping a fully-employed economy into recession.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is at a cyclical low of 4.4% as of June 2017
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is below 5%, S&P 500 20-day returns following a FEDFUNDS increase are not statistically different from returns following no change.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which is the primary driver of the US economy. This translates directly into higher corporate revenues and positive investor sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is ~70% of US GDP
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, sudden increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a high-conviction, short-term recessionary signal. This triggers rapid risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns as investors price in an imminent economic downturn.
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses a similar mechanism for recession dating
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts a positive return over the subsequent 5 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a healthy manufacturing and supply-side of the economy. This supports corporate earnings growth and signals capacity to meet demand, boosting equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The 6-month rolling correlation between INDPRO's 3-month change and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is negative for more than one-third of the testing period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakness in a key interest-rate sensitive sector. This is a leading indicator of a broader slowdown in consumer spending and investment, negatively impacting equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector weakness preceded the 2008 financial crisis
failure_condition: The median 60-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) compared to the unconditional median return.
